name: Eve Apple Tree Thinking System

releases.author: BrandtheBrand
releases.description:
# Eve's Apple Tree Thinking System — v0.4.0

**think how to think**

First public release. Grows your notes into a 3D "tree of light" you can fly around — a thinking tool, not a filing cabinet.

## Features

- **🌳 3D tree of light** — a locked warm-white observatory-style scene built on three.js (bundled, runs fully offline, no network calls).
- **🌲 Forest** — every top-level folder in your vault is its own independent tree (own fields, own trunk, own place in the scene). Zoom out and each tree crossfades into a translucent green silhouette; several folders read as a forest. A Focus tree selector flies you to any one tree or back to the whole forest.
- **🔭 Zoom-driven labels (LOD)** — zoomed out is a clean constellation of dots; zoom in for titles, further for descriptions.
- **🌙 Dark mode** — follows Obsidian's light/dark theme automatically; dots become glowing diamonds on black. A manual 🌙/☀️ override in the panel sticks — it's no longer clobbered by theme changes or by Rebuild.
- **✨ Every dot glows** — a soft halo tinted to each dot's own colour, brightest on ah-has and outputs, tuned separately for light and dark themes. A **Light shine** slider takes it from subtle to strong live; dark mode is where it shows best.
- **👁 Stakeholder views + chair lens** — a note can carry a `## Stakeholder views` section (different "chairs" and their take); picking a chair dims every dot with no view from it, so you can sweep the tree through one perspective at a time.
- **🌉 Cross-tree bridges** — two trees connect only through a bridge note that explains the link, never an automatic cross-tree line.
- **🧲 Link-aware clustering** — dots in the same field that link to each other pull together into clusters; unrelated dots sit apart (toggle in settings, skipped automatically above 500 notes per tree to keep things responsive).
- **🌸 / 🍎 Flower and apple markers** — locate breakthroughs and outputs at a glance; a bloomed/fruited tree shows them on its zoom-out silhouette too.
- **🖱️ Click a dot for its card** — title, description, field, any stakeholder views, and its links to and from other notes, with an **Open note ↗** button that opens the note in a new tab. Bridge nodes open the same way and show their explanation.
- **✋ Hand-placeable dots** — drag any leaf, ah-ha, or output to arrange it; the position saves automatically and survives a rebuild or an app restart. Each dot stays inside its own field wedge, so the layout can't misrepresent a note's field; changing the note's `field` property is how you move a dot between fields. Root and trunk stay fixed, and **⌾ Reset dot layout** returns everything to auto-layout.
- **🎛️ Panel upgrades** — the control panel minimizes to a small pill; a **Text size** slider scales every label live; a **Zoom-out icon** selector picks the far-zoom silhouette shape (Round, Conifer, or Apple).
- Respects `prefers-reduced-motion` (manual auto-spin is exempt by design). Desktop-only — it's a real WebGL scene.

## Install

**Community Plugins browse (pending review):** Settings → Community plugins → Browse → search "Eve Apple Tree Thinking System" → Install → Enable. This path isn't live yet while the listing goes through review.

**Manual install (works now):** download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` from this release's assets, and copy them into `<your-vault>/.obsidian/plugins/eve-apple-tree/`. Then enable it under Settings → Community plugins.

Open the tree from the sprout ribbon icon or the command palette → "Open the tree of light."

## Known limits

- **No live refresh yet.** Add or edit notes, then hit **Rebuild** in the panel. Watching the vault automatically is on the roadmap.
- **Large vaults are guarded, not fully optimized.** Clustering is skipped above 500 notes in a tree; whole-vault ingest gives a friendly notice above ~1500 notes suggesting "Only show notes with tree frontmatter."
- **Desktop only** — no mobile version; the renderer needs real WebGL.
- **Growth axes are fixed** — `field` and `time` are the only two coordinates for now; per-vault custom palettes are on the roadmap.

## Credits

Built by **Evelyn Chan**. MIT licensed — see `LICENSE`. Thanks to everyone who tries it and reports back what's rough.
